hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will increase selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the pharmacophore and cysteine residues. This iteration suggests that further refinement of these positions is necessary to achieve a more significant Δmargin.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing design on these positions allows for targeted disruption of conserved-pocket contacts while preserving pharmacophore regions.

These mutations are proposed to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore region (positions 7-10) or Cys3/Cys14. For example, P1A and P2D are expected to break hydrogen bonding or hydrophobic interactions with off-target receptors.
